q: What is this land all about?
a:
This 120-acre farm has seen a lot — a historic dairy, an eccentric follower of the spiritual leader Ramtha, a golf course, and an organic farm. For the past 20 years, this farm has been known as Talking Horse Ranch, after the famed “talking horse” purchased by previous owner Ernie Kanzler Jr. from the channeler of his spiritual guru, Ramtha.

For these past 20-years the farm has been under-managed, and kept running at a low-idle. Most of the infrastructure is in disrepair, but not beyond the point-of-no-return.

Despite all this, the land is a wonderfully magical place — with excellent topography, landscape, a seasonal creek, 6 ponds, enchanting forests, and incredibly cool and quirky buildings and landscaping. Most of the developed land is south facing slope with excellent sun, the wells produce an incredible 45 and 70 GPM, and the big barn offers 13,000 square feet of usable indoor space. These are just a few of the significant attributes.

40 of the 120 acres will be purchased for Camas Commons, with the other 80 acres entering into perpetual conservation through the San Juan Conservation Land Bank. Together, public access will be included in this plan with a walking trail that connects the Beaverton Marsh Preserve to the top of Beaver Hill, through Camas Commons.
